title: malaxis nemoralis
intro: Malaxis nemoralis, also known as the wood-sorrel or wood-sorrel orchid, is a species of orchid native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and treating various skin conditions, and its extracts have been used in herbal preparations like teas and tinctures to promote wound healing and alleviate respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and safety of Malaxis nemoralis as a medicinal plant.
